channel.title: laurensverspeek.nl — TIL
channel.description: Today I Learned: short notes on things worth remembering.

item.title: A Vue watch dies when its component unmounts
item.content:encoded: If you register a watch inside a composable's setup, Vue binds it to the calling component's effect scope and disposes it on unmount. Guard it with a module flag and it never comes back — your "persist on change" writer silently stops. Run it in a detached effectScope(true) instead and it lives for the whole app.

item.title: Array.slice(-0) returns the whole array
item.content:encoded: arr.slice(-n) takes the last n items — except -0 === 0, so slice(-0) returns everything. A tail -0 that should print nothing prints the lot. Guard n === 0 explicitly before slicing from the end.

item.title: vitest does not type-check your tests
item.content:encoded: vitest run executes tests but never type-checks them. A test with an implicit any, or a .mjs that fails checkJs, passes locally and then breaks CI on a separate vue-tsc/tsc step. Run the type-check steps too before you push.
